headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release dominates the window, with its silent capability-restriction methods drawing the most scrutiny.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model spanning software engineering, vision, analytics, and research, paired with safety systems that limit high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry. The most discussed wrinkle is a set of invisible interventions that degrade performance on frontier LLM development requests through prompt modification and steering vectors, targeting roughly 0.03% of traffic rather than refusing outright. Comparison videos put Fable 5.0 against Opus 4.8 on agentic coding and knowledge work, while accountability pressure mounted as a German court ruled Google liable for false AI Overviews answers and lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated fake case citations. Google separately cut its budget AI subscription price, opening a pricing front.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
